title: Evangelion and the general Anime community
display_title: Evangelion and the general Anime community
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion and the general Anime community             Quote Post by ParanoiaAgent » Tue Sep 20, 2011 11:12 am So ya, I wasn't entirely sure where to post this, or if I should post this at all (seeing as how its more or less based on nothing other than personal opinion), but lately ive noticed a rather.....poor.....conception of Eva in the general anime community. Now, I know every show EVER has had haters; but when every conversation I have in which I bring up Eva on any kind of anime board/chat site/anything involving conversation on the internet ends with me either being called a troll or just a person with bad taste in anime, by everyone, it just feels as if something is off. Is it the same for everyone? I really don't want to sound whiny here, I just honestly don't get it. One of my friends said that he does't like Eva because its a "hipster" anime, like, the anime equivalent of a fedora or something. Is that really how people see Eva now for some reason? If so, why? Sorry to just kinda rant here, just trying to find some input on the matter from non-paranoid people i guess.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Sep 20, 2011 11:16 am I presume for "general anime community" you mean the anime community in the west because Eva is viewed with high regard in Japan and in the parts of Asia where it was shown. And I wonder what the reasons those people give for calling Eva lovers a troll or with bad taste in anime?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Sep 20, 2011 11:42 am Here's a couple of threads that sort of touches on the subject: How could you not like EVA Evangelion: Pretentiously deep? Is there an "Evangelion Backlash" in fandom? NGE = Pretentious? A bit of relevance here: A very fair review of Evangelion I found And a more recent: EVA: The Most Divisive Anime Ever?

[]:             Quote Post by Final Messenger » Tue Sep 20, 2011 11:53 am I’m in the same boat as you (kind of). I had to introduce my friends into eva and yeah they liked it but they never ever want to talk about eva they only ever want to talk about gundam (don’t get me wrong I’m a huge gundam otaku (Eva and gundam are the only series I go full otaku)) and the big three (naruto bleach and one piece) oh and the occasional full metal alchemist conversation. And because of the lack of eva related conversation with my friends I joined here (I‘m really glad I joined eva geeks this place is full of great eva info (it’s how I found out about retake) and other anime related conversation).

[]:             Quote Post by ParanoiaAgent » Tue Sep 20, 2011 8:25 pm Thanks for the reply Ornette, and all the links.....damn my crippling fear of search engines. But ya, it just makes me sorta sad the way people seem to view Eva most of the time. Out of all my friends, ive only managed to convince one to watch Eva. The rest swear they wouldn't like it as they don't have mommy issues.....anyways, im off to read all these wonderful threads, and hopefully come up with a rational reason why Eva would warrant so much hate........

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Tue Sep 20, 2011 9:02 pm NGE, like many a popular or revolutionary show, suffers from the phenomena known as Hype Backlash .

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Sep 20, 2011 9:48 pm > Hype Backlash Still made me wonder why the apparently huge difference in fan reaction in the west and in Japan/Asia. Also it cannot explain why some would not even consider watching the show. As the tvtrope site explains, Hype Backlash came up when you gave in and watched the show, but ParanoiaAgent is saying that he could not even get any person (except one) to start watching the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Sep 20, 2011 10:58 pm Because Eva has, by and large, a horrible fanbase. Not as bad as Naruto or the like, but...pretty bad, maybe two steps above the big SJ titles. Between the pretentious over-reaching tools, the whiners going on about how Shinji just needed to man up and fuck (insert which girl said whiner likes best) here, the idiots who make at least one thread on /a/ a day asking for someone to tell them what EoE meant, the sequel theorists, and V, pretty much nobody on the English-speaking end of the internet hasn't seen some godawful side of Eva fandom. If I'd known about the fandom before I watched the show, odds are I wouldn't have bothered starting it...but I didn't and here we are, eh? Also, fedoras are less a hipster thing and more a "greasy nerd horribly reaching towards what he thinks is classy and fashionable" thing As to the difference in reaction between Japan and the west: 1) the dub is shit but continues to exert a ridiculous degree of influence over people's perception of the characters 2) anime fans in the west are broadly man- and woman-children who can't enjoy a show so flatly critical of them 3) bad fandom leads people to expect the show to be bad; people see what they expect to 4) Evangelion is in many ways directed to Japan and the Japanese people, placing it in a context that the west is less able to relate to (weeaboos excluded) Despite all this, Eva is still among the most popular anime in the western fandom as well (I would guess that it's behind the Big Three, Death Note and maybe Geass and Haruhi), it's just that the popularity is among those who don't exactly give a good representation of the show (same could be said of the other shows I listed, to be honest). tl;dr anime fans are human scum

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Wed Sep 21, 2011 1:18 am Azathoth wrote: 1) the dub is shit but continues to exert a ridiculous degree of influence over people's perception of the characters 2) anime fans in the west are broadly man- and woman-children who can't enjoy a show so flatly critical of them 3) bad fandom leads people to expect the show to be bad; people see what they expect to 4) Evangelion is in many ways directed to Japan and the Japanese people, placing it in a context that the west is less able to relate to (weeaboos excluded)

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Sep 21, 2011 1:42 am I know, right? Don't expect me to argue for it though, I fired my shot and now I'm outta this thread

[]: Re: Evangelion and the general Anime community             Quote Post by CJD » Wed Sep 21, 2011 1:49 am ParanoiaAgent wrote: but when every conversation I have in which I bring up Eva on any kind of anime board/chat site/anything involving conversation on the internet ends with me either being called a troll or just a person with bad taste in anime, by everyone, it just feels as if something is off.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Sep 21, 2011 1:53 am Thanks Azathoth. Your detailed post makes sense to me , particularly the culture gap (e.g. the Shinji character). One thing I want to say is that I notice most Eva fans in Asia do not really think too deeply about the psychology problems of the characters. They take them as just an approach to develop characters but do not link them too personally or emotionally, unlike many experiences I heard from fans in the west. EoE never registers as deeply except those of us old-timer otaku that watched it more than 14 years ago. This may partly explain the huge difference in perception in the west vs in Asia.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Wed Sep 21, 2011 4:36 am For the record, I do agree with most of the points Azathoth makes, except maybe for the bit about the dub. I've frequently seen hardcore fans parade around the notion that the dub is responsible for the misinterpretation of characters in the West, but regardless of its quality (which can and has been debated and nitpicked to kingdom come) I have yet to see any actual evidence of this notion. For what it's worth, neither I nor anyone else I know who's seen the dub first seem to have ever had any major misconceptions about the characters. As for Evangelion 's wider reception and fanbase, I will say that Eva , uniquely among anime, has the capacity both to speak to speak to people on an intensely personal level and, at least partially as a result, to inspire an almost religious fervor among its fans. The nature of the work itself, however, is so immensely dedicated to its own vision and ruthlessly uncompromising in its methods that by the end, most audiences will either be left transformed or left cold, and which is true for each person is largely dependant on individual circumstances, personality and taste. Add to all THAT the fact that most of the people with whom the show's themes resonate most strongly are those who can relate to its expression of loneliness and alienation - in other words, the type of people who are probably not the most positive-minded, socially adept individuals - and I'm sure you can see where things might start to get ugly. Also worthy of note: I find it consistently amusing how no one - no one - hates anime fans more than anime fans. Of course, I'm a self-hating weeaboo-in-denial just like the rest of you, so I'm not going to start pretending I'm above all that.

[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Wed Sep 21, 2011 5:00 am symbv wrote: > Hype Backlash Still made me wonder why the apparently huge difference in fan reaction in the west and in Japan/Asia.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Sep 21, 2011 9:58 am Eva is immensely popular series to this day and was ADV's money printer for the entire era of the company. It's also critically renowned and has been so from 90s to this day. What separates Eva from other popular and critically renowned shows in the west like Cowboy Bebop is that Eva is also very provocative and controversial. Add this together with its reputation and reputation of its fanbase and you have sizable backslash too All in all I think this pic is perfect summary of Eva's reception in internet these days [URL=http://imageshack.us/photo/my-images/838/evathread.jpg/] [/URL]

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Sep 21, 2011 11:29 am Xard wrote: All in all I think this pic is perfect summary of Eva's reception in internet these days

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Sep 21, 2011 1:22 pm A more recent and imo more accurate image would be

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Wed Sep 21, 2011 2:19 pm Evangelion is hated in the west? I just figured with it being on of the highest selling series of all time and something that is considered a classic/must watch series, that it was popular. But that just shows how little I know. Truly a vocal minority of people bitching on the internet properly reflect the community as a whole!

[]: I think I'm becoming Japanese... nah. B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Wed Sep 21, 2011 2:35 pm symbv wrote: Thanks Azathoth. Your detailed post makes sense to me , particularly the culture gap (e.g. the Shinji character).

[]:             Quote Post by SaVaGe » Wed Sep 21, 2011 3:51 pm The term "troll" is used to far too much on forums. Whenever I or someone else brings up Eva on other forums, all the fantatics come out and bath in the nostalgia. This is usually brought about by a Gendo or Shinji referece. It's great.
